audienceQuestion: Why does an AI Agent confidently guess instead of saying it does not know, and how should a user verify the answer before acting?
centralThesis: Reliable AI Agent use requires changing the decision rule from answer at all costs to answer when supported, clarify when underspecified, verify when consequential, and abstain when the truth cannot be determined.

reusableArtifact.en: AI Agent Uncertainty and Fact-Check Checklist
reusableArtifact.zh: AI 智能体不确定性与事实核验清单

P0: Accuracy-only incentives reward guessing over honest uncertainty
Questions may be unavailable, ambiguous, stale, or inherently underdetermined
High-risk claims must be linked to evidence before action
P1: Run an answerability gate before accepting an answer
Separate confirmed facts, inferences, and unknowns
Use primary evidence and independent corroboration for consequential claims
Connect uncertainty to clarify, verify, approve, stop, or abstain
P2: Pretraining sees fluent positive examples without a truth label on every statement
Rare arbitrary facts are difficult to infer from patterns
SimpleQA accuracy, error, and abstention figures illustrate the incentive trade-off
Human approval remains necessary before irreversible high-impact action
P3: Detailed formal proofs and notation from the paper
Model-ranking discussion unrelated to the viewer workflow

chapters.promise: Expose the incentive and preview the reusable checklist
Understand why accuracy can reward the wrong behavior
Decide whether a reliable answer is possible
Make facts, inferences, and unknowns inspectable
Spend evidence effort where errors create consequences
Return to the decision rule and action boundary

factBoundaries: OpenAI source findings and figures are attributed to OpenAI
The checklist is a practical synthesis, not a quoted OpenAI framework
Qualitative confidence labels do not imply calibrated numeric probabilities
The SimpleQA example is not generalized to every model or task

roles.human: frames the question, checks consequence, requests evidence, and approves irreversible action
roles.tinyAgent: states uncertainty, asks for missing context, retrieves evidence, records verification, and stops when evidence is insufficient

contentEdit.reason: Removed items do not add a new viewer decision, fact boundary, verification step, or required source context within the 5-8 minute episode.
